title: The Refrigerator Pack
description: This a pack originally intended to be used amongst friends that avoids (most) electricity/technology mods.
body:
# The Refrigerator Pack 
...is a collection of Forge mods that gravitate around the Create, Tetra and Ars Nouveau mods. There are some QoL and performance improvement additions, and shaderpack support available as well. 

This pack aims to provide a *sort of* pseudo-vanilla experience, with major progression and exploration additions. My goal was to create a pack that avoids most *"traditional"* technology mods. (Mekanism, Applied Energistics, Immersive Engineering, etc.)

## Installation

### Modrinth App
- You can either:

  **a.)** Download and play the modpack directly in the official Modrinth App

  **b.)** Download the *.mrpack* file and create a new instance "from file" in the Modrinth App

### Third-party
- To install and play the modpack with the *.mrpack* file, follow the instructions [here](https://docs.modrinth.com/modpacks/play).

### Server Side
- A lot of the client-side mods will make a server crash on startup due to complications regarding what some of said mods will do *in a client* that conflict with server processes.
- To install the server-side mods, there is a [release](https://modrinth.com/modpack/the-refrigerator-pack/versions) known as (pack name) x.x_server; directions to install (into a standard port-forwarded Forge 1.20.1 server) are listed below:

1. Follow option "**a.)**" above to install the modpack and click on the icon (**not the ▷ button**).
2. Select `📁 Folder` to browse its files.
3. Go into the "mods" folder.
4. Select all (ctrl + a), then press ctrl + c to copy.
5. Go into your server's "mods" folder.
6. Right click (or ctrl + v) to paste.
- Your server should now be able to run properly and can be joined using the normal (x.x ~~_server~~) version of the modpack.
- To install into any third-party hosted server, or any potential other type of server supporting Forge 1.20.1, follow steps 1-3, then add the mods by name, manually.
  - `let me know if there is any other method because this one seems painstaking and, frankly, it sucks.`

## Resources

### Modlist 
- ...can be found on [Moddermore](https://moddermore.net/list/sPD8Y3jh8uxn).

### Resource Packs

- If you want a decently vanilla-themed resource pack, I recommend VanillaTweaks.

- For PBR stuff, I personally have had mixed results with DefaultPBR.
